subject: 🏃 Tuesday — Easy 3 mi (soft surface)
html:
Today's workout: Easy 3 miles on a soft surface. Sawyer Camp Trail or Bayfront paths — no pavement. Target pace 9:30–10:00/mi, HR under 140. This is not a fitness run; it's a diagnostic run. Your only job is to observe how the knee responds under the new PT-informed plan.

Before you head out, do the daily mobility flush (~12 min): Foam roller T-spine extensions (8–10 reps), standing lunges with hip IR + extension (8–10 reps each side), world's greatest stretch (5 each side), 90/90 hip stretch with IR emphasis (60 sec each side), half-kneeling couch stretch (60 sec each side), hip CARs (3 each direction, each leg), cat-cow (10 reps).

Focus cue: Notice your knee at each mile marker — Green, Yellow, or Red. If it talks at all, walk. No negotiating. This is data collection, not training.

Where you are: Day 2 of Week 1, Chassis Build — 96 days to race day, 24 days to PT follow-up on May 15. Your last run (April 16) flagged Yellow afterward, and the PT diagnosed medial patellofemoral joint irritation driven by bilateral hip IR limitation and hip/glute weakness. Yesterday's Hip & Core A session started the rebuild. Today's 3-miler is the first run under the new framework — keep it boring and report back how the knee felt during and after.
